title: Flow-Matched Marquetry
seed_problem: source veneer sheet with a smooth grain-direction field + target polyomino panel with a desired flow field; solver places non-overlapping piece poses on the sheet AND poses tiling the panel, scored on seam grain mismatch + waste - shape diversity
mutation: composite: each output object is ONE physical piece carrying TWO independently chosen poses (a sheet-cut pose and a panel-glue pose) whose rotation DIFFERENCE alone determines the glued-in grain angle, coupling a sheet packing and a panel packing through every piece; the objective additionally rewards distinct-shape usage, composing dual-packing-coupling + grain-flow-continuity + piece-shape-diversity into one score.
strategies: trivial: place zero piece instances (reproduces the checker's own do-nothing baseline)

area-first greedy: tile the panel largest-shape-first by geometric fit alone, cut each piece from the sheet by blind first-fit (rotation 0 preferred, then row-major position) -- fully ignores both grain fields

pose-pair search (strong): for every accepted panel placement, exhaustively search the sheet over ALL (position, sheet-rotation) pairs and keep the one whose net-rotation-shifted angles best match the panel's local desired flow field; gate piece size by how much the target field varies across the candidate footprint (small/diverse shapes in curved regions, large shapes in flat regions)
general solver headroom: jointly optimize panel-side rotation too (not just geometric fit), or use local search / simulated annealing over the whole instance list to escape the row-major scan order
